preview_text:
The model favors Algeria at 45%, with Jordan at 25% and a draw at 30%. Algeria carries a clear advantage, though the model assigns Jordan a meaningful path to victory and sees a draw as the second-most-likely outcome.

The 20-percentage-point gap between Algeria and Jordan reflects competitive but asymmetric match dynamics. The relatively high draw probability—nearly one-third—indicates the model expects a closely contested encounter where neither side dominates decisively. Algeria's win probability exceeds 40%, suggesting stronger underlying metrics, yet the spread leaves room for upset or stalemate.

The model and market diverge noticeably. Sportsbooks price Algeria 18 percentage points higher (63% vs. 45%), while assigning Jordan only 14% (11 points below the model) and draws 8 points lower. Polymarket consensus tracks sportsbooks closely. The dashboard makes no claim about which reading is correct; the gap warrants attention for those calibrating their own forecasts.
